source.description: R-wave detections derived from the ECG channel of the Medicom MTD ECG and respiration recording.

processing: r_wave_times contains ECG R-wave occurrence times derived from the parent ECG signal.
r_wave_samples contains the corresponding sample positions on the parent ECG sample grid.
dtk is derived from successive r_wave_times and remains generic interval data.
The leading NaN in dtk preserves alignment between R-wave times and intervals.
Algorithms consuming dtk must apply their specified missing-value behavior.

source.description: Simultaneous ECG and respiration recording acquired with a Medicom MTD system. ECG was recorded with electrodes and respiration was recorded with a chest belt.

notes: The simultaneous respiration channel can support future scientific validation of ECG-derived respiration, such as comparing dominant respiratory frequencies between EDR and reference respiration.

source.description: Synthetic interval series containing a non-finite interval for hrv.tdmetrics invalid-value conformance.

processing: Contains a positive interval sequence with one Inf value.
The Inf value is invalid for hrv.tdmetrics and is retained to test rejection of non-finite intervals.

source.description: Small hand-calculated expected EDR outputs for ecg.sloperange conformance cases.

processing: positive_edr contains the analytical EDR sequence [5, 7, 6] for the small positive synthetic case.
boundary_edr contains [NaN, 7, NaN] to preserve alignment when the first and last R-wave windows are incomplete.

source.description: Small synthetic medfilt_threshold input and expected-output columns generated from the current Biosigmat medfiltThreshold implementation or derived analytically from the normative contract.

processing: Reference outputs were generated with Biosigmat medfiltThreshold at commit 04365c8ac02c886c4839412a319cac60221ab1ef.
threshold_normal uses window = 3, factor = 2, max_threshold = 5 for x_normal.
threshold_window_larger uses window = 99, factor = 2, max_threshold = 100 for x_normal.
threshold_window_4 and threshold_window_5 use x_odd_even with factor = 1 and max_threshold = 1000.
threshold_cap uses x_normal with window = 3, factor = 10, max_threshold = 4.
threshold_include_nan_window is an analytical expected output for include-NaN median semantics with window = 2, factor = 2, and max_threshold = 100.

source.description: Small lpd_filter coefficient reference columns generated from the current Biosigmat lpdfilter implementation.

processing: Reference outputs were generated with Biosigmat lpdfilter at commit 04365c8ac02c886c4839412a319cac60221ab1ef.
coefficient_default_order_4 uses sampling_frequency = 256 Hz, stop_frequency = 12 Hz, omitted pass_frequency, and order = 4.
coefficient_pass10_order_4 uses sampling_frequency = 256 Hz, stop_frequency = 12 Hz, pass_frequency = 10 Hz, and order = 4.

source.description: Small synthetic NaN-filtering inputs and expected outputs generated from the current Biosigmat nanfilter and nanfiltfilt implementations or derived analytically from the normative contract.

processing: Reference outputs were generated with Biosigmat nanfilter and nanfiltfilt at commit 04365c8ac02c886c4839412a319cac60221ab1ef.
All cases use numerator_coefficients = [0.5, 0.5], denominator_coefficients = [1], and max_gap = 1 unless a case explicitly omits max_gap.
pulse_case_table covers no-NaN, short-gap, and long-gap behavior.
nan_filter_boundary_nan and nan_filtfilt_boundary_nan are analytical identity-filter expected outputs for boundary-NaN preservation.
short_segments_case_table covers candidate finite segments too short for the selected filtering operation; the expected outputs are analytical all-NaN results under the current contract.

source.description: Small synthetic snap_to_peak expected-output columns generated from the current Biosigmat snaptopeak implementation, plus analytical invalid-value inputs.

processing: Reference outputs were generated with Biosigmat snaptopeak at commit 04365c8ac02c886c4839412a319cac60221ab1ef.
case_table covers local-maximum snapping and boundary-clipped search windows.
window_case_table covers a case where changing window_size changes the selected maximum.
invalid_ecg_nan_table and invalid_detections_nan_table contain analytical invalid-value inputs for NaN rejection.
